perturbation.original_text: 1.5 Operation of Smaaash Centres. Franchisee or third party sub -franchisees shall be under an obligation to set up at least 6 (six) Smaaash Centres during the first Contract Year or any other time period as may be provided by Franchisor.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 Operation of Smaaash Centres. Franchisee or third party sub -franchisees shall be under an obligation to set up at least 6 (six) Smaaash Centres during the first Contract Year. The Franchisor holds the right to demand up to 10 Smaaash Centres within the first contract year, after giving a 30 day notice to the Franchisee.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces a contradiction regarding the number of Smaaash Centres required within the first contract year. Originally, it was fixed at 'at least 6.' The modification allows the Franchisor to demand up to 10, creating uncertainty and a potential conflict in expectations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.5 Operation of Smaaash Centres. Franchisee or third party sub -franchisees shall be under an obligation to set up at least 6 (six) Smaaash Centres during the first Contract Year. The Franchisor holds the right to demand up to 10 Smaaash Centres within the first contract year, after giving a 30 day notice to the Franchisee. The Smaaash Centres shall be established and operated in the Territory using the assumed trade name 'Smaaash'or any other trade name that the Franchisor may designate.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by allowing the Franchisor to demand up to 10 Smaaash Centres, while the original text and the contradicted text state that the Franchisee is obligated to set up at least 6. This creates uncertainty about the Franchisee's obligation, which now can be anywhere from 6 to 10, leading to a direct conflict regarding the number of centers required, as defined in 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2 Promotional Material and Products. Franchisee shall submit to Franchisor, for Franchisor's prior written approval, samples of all advertising and promotional materials that Franchisee desires to use to promote Smaaash Centres, including without limitation, print and online advertising designs, trade show display materials, press releases and interviews for publication in any media ("Promotional Material").
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 Promotional Material and Products. Franchisee shall submit to Franchisor, for Franchisor's prior written approval, samples of all advertising and promotional materials that Franchisee desires to use to promote Smaaash Centres, including without limitation, print and online advertising designs, trade show display materials, press releases and interviews for publication in any media ("Promotional Material"). However, the Franchisee may use any promotional material they deem fit for the first 3 months of the first Contract Year.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence introduces a contradiction to the Franchisee's obligations to submit promotional materials for prior approval. The original text dictates all materials need approval; the new sentence allows complete autonomy for 3 months, creating an in-text contradiction on when approval is required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2 Promotional Material and Products. Franchisee shall submit to Franchisor, for Franchisor's prior written approval, samples of all advertising and promotional materials that Franchisee desires to use to promote Smaaash Centres, including without limitation, print and online advertising designs, trade show display materials, press releases and interviews for publication in any media ("Promotional Material"). However, the Franchisee may use any promotional material they deem fit for the first 3 months of the first Contract Year. Franchisee shall modify any disapproved Promotional Material to satisfy Franchisor's reasonable objections so that it is acceptable to Franchisor.
perturbation.justification: The original text of section 5.2 states that the Franchisee shall submit all promotional materials to the Franchisor for prior written approval. The changed text adds a sentence that allows the Franchisee to use any promotional material they deem fit for the first 3 months of the first Contract Year. The contradiction lies in whether the Franchisee needs to submit promotional materials for approval during the first 3 months of the first contract year. The original text dictates that they do, while the added text dictates they don't, creating a direct in-text contradiction within section 5.2 itself. This adheres to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract (in this case, different sentences within the same section) provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 10.11 Assignment. The Franchisor shall be entitled to assign, transfer, encumber or dispose of any of its rights and or obligations under this Agreement, including to an affiliate, without the prior written consent of the Franchisee. The Franchisee shall not be entitled to assign, transfer, encumber or dispose of any of its rights and or obligations under this Agreement, including to an affiliate, without the prior written consent of the Franchisor.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.11 Assignment. The Franchisor shall be entitled to assign, transfer, encumber or dispose of any of its rights and or obligations under this Agreement, including to an affiliate, without the prior written consent of the Franchisee. The Franchisee shall be entitled to assign, transfer, encumber or dispose of any of its rights and or obligations under this Agreement, including to an affiliate, given that the new entity, complies with the obligations that are currently held by the Franchisee under this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modification alters the Franchisee's ability to assign their obligations. Originally, this required Franchisor consent. Now, assignment is permitted if the new entity simply complies with the existing obligations. This introduces an in-text contradiction since there is no need for prior written consent, so the Franchisor loses its authority over who takes over as franchisee.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.11 Assignment. The Franchisor shall be entitled to assign, transfer, encumber or dispose of any of its rights and or obligations under this Agreement, including to an affiliate, without the prior written consent of the Franchisee. The Franchisee shall be entitled to assign, transfer, encumber or dispose of any of its rights and or obligations under this Agreement, including to an affiliate, given that the new entity, complies with the obligations that are currently held by the Franchisee under this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the Franchisee's ability to assign their obligations. Originally, this required Franchisor consent. Now, assignment is permitted if the new entity simply complies with the existing obligations. This introduces an in-text contradiction since there is no need for prior written consent, so the Franchisor loses its authority over who takes over as franchisee. The changed text and contradicted text are in the same location but the modified text allows for assignment without the Franchisor's consent, contradicting the original requirement for consent.
